factors.summary: Demand is accelerating in several segments.

evidence.text: Textron Aviation's revenues increased $40 million, 4%, in the first quarter of 2016, primarily due to higher Citation jet volume and mix. Bell's segment profit increased $6 million, 8%, in the first quarter of 2016, primarily due to improved performance, largely driven by favorable program adjustments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows revenue increases due to higher volume and mix, indicating accelerating demand.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Textron Systems Revenues and Operating Expenses: Revenues at Textron Systems increased $9 million, 3%, in the first quarter of 2016, primarily due to higher volume of $46 million in the Unmanned Systems product line, partially offset by lower volume of $42 million in the Weapons and Sensors product line.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments with increased revenues in unmanned systems.

evidence.text: Textron Aviation's revenues increased $40 million, 4%, in the first quarter of 2016, compared with the first quarter of 2015, primarily due to higher Citation jet volume and mix. Bell's segment profit increased $6 million, 8%, in the first quarter of 2016, compared with the first quarter of 2015, primarily due to improved performance, largely driven by favorable program adjustments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue and profit increases across segments show accelerating organic growth.

evidence.text: Textron Aviation delivered 34 Citation jets and 26 King Air turboprops in the first quarter of 2016, compared with 33 Citation jets and 25 King Air turboprops in the first quarter of 2015. Bell delivered 10 H-1 aircraft in the first quarter of 2016, compared with 4 H-1 aircraft in the first quarter of 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased unit deliveries across product lines indicate growing volumes.

factors.summary: End markets are growing, particularly in government and commercial segments.

evidence.text: Bell's major U.S. Government programs at this time are the V-22 tiltrotor aircraft and the H-1 helicopter platforms, which are both in the production stage and represent a significant portion of Bell's revenues from the U.S. Government. Textron Aviation's portion of the segment's revenues derived from aftermarket sales and services represented 35% of its total revenues in both the first quarter of 2016 and 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in government and commercial segments supports strong end-market momentum.
